message:
feat(tui): data_dir injection, state persistence, clippy fixes

- Wire data_dir_override through run_tui_ftui for testable state paths
- Add --reset-state handler to clear persisted TUI state
- ThemeEditorState::from_data_dir accepts injected path
- Fix clippy bool_assert_comparison (assert_eq with literal bool)
- Fix cargo fmt formatting in saved_views serialization
- Includes concurrent agent changes: analytics query improvements,
  chart data fixes, tui smoke test updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2w4","title":"P1.1 Define Source/Origin Rust types","description":"# Define Source/Origin Rust Types

## Context
We need Rust types to represent "where a conversation came from" - the provenance system. These types will be used throughout cass: storage, indexing, search, CLI, TUI.

## Location
Create new module: src/model/source.rs (or src/sources.rs if we prefer top-level)

## Types to Define

### SourceKind enum
\`\`\`rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum SourceKind {
    Local,
    Ssh,
    // Future: S3, Git, Http
}
\`\`\`

### Source struct (configuration-level)
\`\`\`rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Source {
    /// Stable, user-friendly identifier (e.g., "local", "work-laptop", "home-server")
    pub id: String,
    
    /// What type of source this is
    pub kind: SourceKind,
    
    /// Display label for UI (often SSH alias or hostname)
    pub host_label: Option<String>,
    
    /// Optional stable machine identifier (hashed if desired)
    pub machine_id: Option<String>,
    
    /// Platform hint for path defaults
    pub platform: Option<Platform>,
    
    /// Arbitrary config (SSH params, path rewrites, etc)
    pub config: serde_json::Value,
    
    pub created_at: Option<i64>,
    pub updated_at: Option<i64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum Platform {
    Macos,
    Linux,
    Windows,
}
\`\`\`

### Origin struct (per-conversation metadata)
\`\`\`rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Origin {
    /// References Source.id
    pub source_id: String,
    
    /// Denormalized for convenience
    pub kind: SourceKind,
    
    /// Display host label (may differ from source's host_label)
    pub host: Option<String>,
}

impl Default for Origin {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            source_id: "local".into(),
            kind: SourceKind::Local,
            host: None,
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Constants
\`\`\`rust
pub const LOCAL_SOURCE_ID: &str = "local";
\`\`\`

## Considerations
- Use serde for JSON serialization (robot output compatibility)
- Derive common traits: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash where appropriate
- Document public types with doc comments
- Consider implementing Display for user-friendly formatting

## Wire into lib.rs
Export these types from the crate root so they're accessible:
\`\`\`rust
pub mod sources;
pub use sources::{Source, SourceKind, Origin, Platform, LOCAL_SOURCE_ID};
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Types defined with proper derives
- [ ] Doc comments on all public items
- [ ] Unit tests for Default impls and serialization
- [ ] Exported from crate root
- [ ] cargo check passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:54:10.114500Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T06:51:13.559723Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T06:51:13.559723Z","close_reason":"Implemented SourceKind, Source, Origin, SourceFilter types with LOCAL_SOURCE_ID constant in src/sources/provenance.rs. Refactored config.rs to use shared SourceKind instead of duplicate SourceConnectionType. 34 unit tests pass. cargo check/clippy/fmt all pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2w4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-495","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2w98","title":"[Task] Remaining Connectors Edge Case Tests","description":"## Task: Remaining Connectors Edge Case Tests

Apply the edge case test pattern established in Claude connector to ALL remaining connectors.

### Connectors to Cover
- [ ] `src/connectors/cursor.rs` - Cursor IDE sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/aider.rs` - Aider CLI sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/cline.rs` - Cline plugin sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/amp.rs` - Amp sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/opencode.rs` - OpenCode sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/pi_agent.rs` - Pi-Agent sessions
- [ ] `src/connectors/goose.rs` - Goose sessions

### Per-Connector Test Requirements
Each connector must have:
1. **Standard edge cases** (10 tests from Claude pattern):
   - Truncated JSON, Invalid UTF-8, Empty file, etc.
2. **Connector-specific edge cases** (3-5 tests):
   - Format quirks unique to that agent

### Implementation Approach
1. Create `#[cfg(test)] mod edge_case_tests` in each file
2. Copy standard test helpers from Claude
3. Add connector-specific cases

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 connectors have edge case test modules
- [ ] All tests pass: `cargo test connectors::*::edge_case_tests`
- [ ] No panics on any malformed input across all connectors
- [ ] Each connector has 10+ edge case tests

### Verification
```bash
cargo test edge_case_tests -- --nocapture 2>&1 | grep -E "(test result|PASSED|FAILED)"
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:03:39.854112873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:49:39.520299360Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:49:39.520231203Z","close_reason":"Completed: Edge case tests added to all 6 existing connectors (goose.rs doesn't exist).  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2xq0","title":"Remove duplicate PhaseTracker definitions, use shared module","description":"## Priority 0 (CRITICAL): Remove PhaseTracker Duplicates

- tests/e2e_cli_flows.rs ✓ (has `use util::e2e_log::PhaseTracker`)

**Files with DUPLICATE local PhaseTracker (MUST FIX):**
- tests/e2e_large_dataset.rs (line ~29: `struct PhaseTracker`)
- tests/e2e_pages.rs (line ~58: `struct PhaseTracker`)
- tests/e2e_search_index.rs (line ~29: `struct PhaseTracker`)

**Files using E2eLogger but NOT PhaseTracker (separate beads):**
- tests/e2e_filters.rs
- tests/e2e_index_tui.rs
- tests/e2e_install_easy.rs
- tests/e2e_multi_connector.rs
- tests/e2e_sources.rs

### Required Changes (3 files only)

1. **tests/e2e_large_dataset.rs:**
   - DELETE local `struct PhaseTracker` and `impl PhaseTracker`
   - ADD `use util::e2e_log::PhaseTracker;`

2. **tests/e2e_pages.rs:**
   - DELETE local `struct PhaseTracker` and `impl PhaseTracker`
   - ADD `use util::e2e_log::PhaseTracker;`

3. **tests/e2e_search_index.rs:**
   - DELETE local `struct PhaseTracker` and `impl PhaseTracker`
   - ADD `use util::e2e_log::PhaseTracker;`

### Testing Requirements

```bash
# Verify compilation
cargo test --test e2e_large_dataset --no-run
cargo test --test e2e_pages --no-run
cargo test --test e2e_search_index --no-run

# Run tests
cargo test --test e2e_large_dataset
cargo test --test e2e_pages
cargo test --test e2e_search_index

# Verify JSONL output
E2E_LOG=1 cargo test --test e2e_pages -- --nocapture
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event | test("phase"))'
```

### Verification Command
```bash
# This should return ONLY the shared definition (1 match)
grep -r 'struct PhaseTracker' tests/
# Expected output: tests/util/e2e_log.rs:pub struct PhaseTracker {
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 0 local PhaseTracker definitions in test files (only 1 in util/e2e_log.rs)
- [ ] All 3 files import from util::e2e_log::PhaseTracker
- [ ] All 3 tests compile without errors
- [ ] All 3 tests pass
- [ ] JSONL output format unchanged (phase_start, phase_end events)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:03:45.816505346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T18:36:03.697382013Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T18:36:03.697309147Z","close_reason":"Consolidated PhaseTracker into tests/util/e2e_log.rs and updated E2E tests to use shared tracker; added drop safety and fixed beads JSONL corruption","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2yg2","title":"Status bar and toast notifications for semantic","description":"## Purpose
Visual feedback for semantic search state in TUI.

## Status Bar Elements
- Mode indicator: mode:LEX / mode:SEM / mode:SEM* / mode:HYB
- Download progress (when active): ⬇️ 45%
- Optional embedder info: emb:minilm

## Toast Notifications
- "Semantic search ready" - ML model becomes available
- "Semantic index rebuilt" - after index upgrade  
- "Download failed: {reason}" - on error with retry info
- "Using hash fallback" - when switching to hash mode

## Toast Behavior
- Auto-dismiss after 3 seconds
- Don't stack more than 2 toasts
- Newer toast replaces older

## Color Coding
- LEX: default/white
- SEM: cyan
- SEM*: cyan (asterisk distinguishes hash)
- HYB: magenta

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status bar shows correct mode
- [ ] Download progress visible during download
- [ ] Toasts appear and auto-dismiss
- [ ] No UI glitches during state changes

## Depends On
- tui.sem.state (State machine)

## References
- Plan: Section 7.4 Status Bar Display","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T01:27:07.494875Z","updated_at":"2026-01-05T22:59:36.419263859Z","closed_at":"2026-01-05T18:47:07.187252Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2yg2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-vh6q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-8q8f","title":"Canonicalization pipeline","description":"## Purpose
Implement text canonicalization for consistent embedding input quality.

## Background
Raw agent logs contain noise that hurts embedding quality:
- Markdown formatting (**bold**, [links](url))
- Huge code blocks with repetitive patterns
- Tool call transcripts, progress indicators

Canonicalization produces clean, consistent text. CRITICAL: Must be deterministic for content hashing!

## Algorithm
1. **Unicode normalize (NFC)** ← CRITICAL: ensures visually identical strings hash identically
2. Strip markdown formatting (keep text content)
3. Collapse code blocks: first 20 + last 10 lines, [code omitted] in middle
4. Normalize whitespace (collapse runs, trim)
5. Filter low-signal content ("OK", "Done.")
6. Truncate to MAX_EMBED_CHARS (default 2000)

## Why Unicode Normalization Matters
Without NFC normalization:
- "café" (4 chars, precomposed) ≠ "café" (5 chars, e + combining accent)
- These look identical but produce different hashes
- Would cause duplicate embeddings or missed incremental updates

## Config
- CASS_SEM_MAX_CHARS=2000
- CASS_SEM_CODE_HEAD_LINES=20
- CASS_SEM_CODE_TAIL_LINES=10

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unicode NFC normalization applied first
- [ ] canonicalize_for_embedding(raw) -> String
- [ ] content_hash(raw) uses canonical text
- [ ] Deterministic (same visual input = same output)
- [ ] Handles edge cases (empty, all-code, unicode combining chars)
- [ ] Test: "café" (precomposed) == "café" (decomposed) after canonicalization

## Depends On
- sem.emb.trait

## References
- Plan: Section 4.6 Canonicalization","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T01:23:10.741631Z","updated_at":"2026-01-05T22:59:36.430912919Z","closed_at":"2026-01-05T16:04:26.928Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-8q8f","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-vmet","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-8uw2","title":"[BASELINE] Record Performance Baselines Before Optimization","description":"## Overview (from PLAN Section 1.A and Section 2)

Before implementing ANY optimization, we must record comprehensive baseline metrics. This ensures we can accurately measure improvement and detect regressions.

## Required Baseline Measurements

### 1. Benchmark Suite (Section 2.1)
Run and record these benchmarks:
```bash
cargo bench --bench search_perf -- --save-baseline main
cargo bench --bench index_perf -- --save-baseline main
cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- --save-baseline main
```

**Key Metrics to Record:**
| Benchmark | Current p50 | Target |
|-----------|-------------|--------|
| `vector_index_search_50k` | 56.1 ms | 2-3 ms |
| `vector_index_search_10k` | 11.2 ms | <1 ms |
| `search_latency` (40 convs) | 10.5 µs | <5 µs |
| `wildcard_large_dataset/substring` | 7.5 ms | <2 ms |
| `canonicalize_long_message` | 951 µs | ~300 µs |
| `index_small_batch` | 13.3 ms | maintain |

### 2. Indexing Metrics (Section 2.2)
For a corpus of 3000 conversations × 12 messages = 36,000 messages:
- `elapsed_ms`: p50/p95/p99
- wall clock time
- throughput (messages/s)
- peak RSS (MB)

```bash
# Generate profiling corpus
cargo run --release -- index --full 2>&1 | tee /tmp/index_baseline.log
```

### 3. Search Latency by Query Type (Section 2.3)
Run N=200 iterations for each query type:
| Query Type | p50 | p95 | p99 |
|-----------|-----|-----|-----|
| exact (`serialize`) | 3ms | 4ms | 4ms |
| prefix (`ser*`) | 3ms | 3ms | 4ms |
| suffix (`*ialize`) | 6ms | 7ms | 7ms |
| substring (`*erial*`) | 9ms | 10ms | 10ms |
| phrase (`"serialize benchmark"`) | 3ms | 4ms | 4ms |

**IMPORTANT**: CLI-per-search includes cold-open costs. Need to separate `open_ms` vs `query_ms`.

## Recording Location

Store baseline results in:
- Git tag: `perf-baseline-round1`
- Criterion baselines: `target/criterion/*/main/`
- Manual measurements: `docs/perf/baseline_round1.md`

## When to Run

1. **Before starting ANY P0 optimization**
2. **After each optimization** (to create new baseline)
3. **Before creating a PR** (to include before/after comparison)

## Dependencies
- Must complete BEFORE any optimization implementation begins
- Part of Epic: coding_agent_session_search-rq7z","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T03:41:41.868679014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T06:22:46.913538385Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T06:22:46.913538385Z","close_reason":"Baseline benchmarks recorded in docs/perf/baseline_round1.md. Key results: vector_index_search_50k=57.76ms (target: 2-3ms), lexical search_latency=10.7µs, wildcard_large_dataset/substring=6.19ms. Criterion baselines saved to target/criterion/*/main/.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9210b91f","title":"Dry-Run Mode (--dry-run)","description":"# Dry-Run Mode (--dry-run)

## Problem Statement
Agents want to validate queries without executing them:
- Is the syntax valid?
- How will it be interpreted?
- What would the cost be?

Currently, the only way to check is to run the query and examine results.

## Proposed Solution
Add `--dry-run` flag that validates and analyzes without executing:
```bash
cass search "complex query" --dry-run --json
```

Output:
```json
{
  "dry_run": true,
  "valid": true,
  "explanation": {...},
  "estimated_results": "10-100",
  "estimated_cost_ms": "<50",
  "warnings": []
}
```

## Design Decisions

### What Dry-Run Does
1. Parse the query (full validation)
2. Analyze filters and constraints
3. Estimate result count (if possible)
4. Estimate execution time
5. Return without actually searching

### What Dry-Run Does NOT Do
- Access the search index
- Return actual results
- Modify any state

### Estimation Accuracy
Result count estimation could use:
- Index statistics (total documents, term frequencies)
- Historical query patterns
- Simple heuristics

Don't over-engineer; rough estimates are valuable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--dry-run` returns without executing search
- [ ] Invalid queries return `valid: false` with error details
- [ ] Valid queries include explanation
- [ ] Response time is <10ms (no index access)
- [ ] Works with all query types

## Effort Estimate
Low-Medium - 2-3 hours. Query parsing is already done; this skips execution.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234185Z","updated_at":"2025-12-15T06:23:15.005415254Z","closed_at":"2025-12-02T05:06:06.678765Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-94f61ee9","title":"Status Command (cass status)","description":"# Status Command (cass status)

## Problem Statement
Agents need a quick way to check system health before running searches:
- Is the index fresh?
- How many sessions are indexed?
- Are there pending updates?

Currently requires running `cass diag` which is verbose and slow.

## Proposed Solution
Add lightweight `cass status` command:
```bash
cass status --json
```

Output:
```json
{
  "healthy": true,
  "index": {
    "fresh": false,
    "last_indexed_at": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z",
    "age_seconds": 3600,
    "stale": true,
    "stale_threshold_seconds": 1800
  },
  "database": {
    "conversations": 150,
    "messages": 4500
  },
  "pending": {
    "new_sessions": 5,
    "modified_sessions": 2,
    "connectors_with_updates": ["claude", "codex"]
  },
  "cache": {
    "entries": 47,
    "hit_rate": 0.85
  },
  "recommended_action": "run 'cass index' to update 7 sessions"
}
```

## Design Decisions

### Staleness Definition
Index is "stale" if:
- `last_indexed_at` is older than threshold (default 30 minutes)
- There are pending sessions detected via watch_state

### Performance
Status should be FAST (<100ms):
- Read watch_state.json for pending detection
- Read meta table for last_indexed_at
- Read cache stats from memory
- NO index scanning

### Recommended Action
Include human/agent-readable suggestion based on state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cass status --json` returns health summary
- [ ] Response time <100ms
- [ ] `fresh` accurately reflects index state
- [ ] `pending` shows sessions awaiting indexing
- [ ] `recommended_action` provides actionable guidance
- [ ] Works without --json for human-readable output

## Effort Estimate
Medium - 3-4 hours. Requires aggregating state from multiple sources.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234185Z","updated_at":"2025-12-01T02:06:28.469087590Z","closed_at":"2025-12-01T02:06:28.469087590Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-94pe","title":"Model management system (download, verify, state)","description":"## Purpose
Complete model management: manifest, state machine, download, verification, upgrades.

## Model Manifest (models.manifest.toml)
```toml
[[models]]
id = "all-minilm-l6-v2"
repo = "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"

files = [

## State Machine
```rust
pub enum ModelState {
    NotInstalled,
    NeedsConsent,
    Downloading { progress_pct: u8, bytes: u64, total: u64 },
    Verifying,
    Ready,
    Disabled { reason: String },
    VerificationFailed { reason: String },
    UpdateAvailable { current: String, latest: String }, // ← ADDED
}
```

## Model Version Upgrades
When we ship a new model version (e.g., better model, security fix):
1. Update models.manifest.toml in new cass release
2. On startup, compare installed model revision vs manifest
3. If mismatch: set state to UpdateAvailable (notify only, don't auto-upgrade)
4. User runs `cass models install --upgrade` to update
5. After model update, vector index is automatically rebuilt

Why notify-only? Auto-upgrading could:
- Use bandwidth unexpectedly
- Break workflows if new model has different behavior
- Cause confusion if results change

## Download System
- Resumable (HTTP Range header)
- Progress reporting via channel
- Exponential backoff (3 retries, 5s → 15s → 45s)
- Timeout: 5 minutes per file
- Clean up partial files on cancel/failure

## Verification + Atomic Install
- Download to models/<name>.downloading/
- Verify SHA256 for each file
- Atomic rename to models/<name>/
- Write .verified marker with timestamp + revision

## Index Migration on Model Change
When model revision changes:
1. Detect: installed model revision != index metadata embedder_revision
2. Mark semantic as unavailable temporarily
3. Rebuild vector index in background
4. Notify user: "Rebuilding semantic index for new model..."
5. When complete, semantic becomes available

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full download → verify → install flow
- [ ] Partial download resumes correctly
- [ ] Corrupt download detected and retried
- [ ] State transitions are correct
- [ ] No network without explicit consent
- [ ] Model version mismatch detected on startup
- [ ] Index rebuild triggered after model upgrade
- [ ] Cancel download cleans up partial files

## Depends On
- sem.emb.ml (FastEmbed embedder)
